entry.title: [SGS] Did Bam Adebayo punch Tyler Herro in the face because Herro “doesn’t believe in history?”
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with dear friend of the show, senior NBA reporter/writer Fred Katz! The gals talk to Fred about the biggest stories in the NBA from Summer League and free agency.
First up: Mitchell Robinson was traded from the Knicks to the Celtics, and he is now trolling both Celtics fans and his former teammates. Fred describes Mitch’s persona and tells behind-the-scenes stories about him (including some insights from his forthcoming book about the Knicks!) and everyone spends a surprising amount of time talking about fonts.
Then, the punch heard round the NBA! Bam Adabayo punched his former teammate Tyler Herro in the face at 9am at Summer League in Las Vegas — is it because Tyler Herro “doesn’t believe in history” or anything that happened before 1950? And, finally, what is the angle that LeBron James’s camp is trying to spin with the Sixers talk? Is it real? Or is he just going to Golden State? 0:00 The gals say hello 2:15 Fred Katz joins the show! 6:28 Mitchell Robinson traded 31:16 Bam Adebayo-Tyler Herro fight 46:05 LeBron to Sixers? 1:00:06 Fashion mailbag

entry.title: [SGS] Argentina is at the center of World Cup conspiracy theories and an FBI investigation
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back! First, they talk about the lawsuit that uncovered a video Phillies star Bryce Harper made for a FanDuel customer — and Harper’s PR response.
Then, the gals dive into World Cup drama. First, the response to racist remarks by Spain’s former prime minister. Then, they talk about France vs. Spain, and why the US men’s soccer program needs to learn from those countries. Then, a discussion about the controversial calls that led to England and Argentina making it to the semi-finals (involves tin-foil hat theories). The gals also discuss England star Jude Bellingham and his coach, and decide that VAR is the LinkedIn of sports.
Finally, the gals talk about Argentina, because the country’s soccer federation is at the center of an FBI investigation in South Florida.

entry.title: [SGS] Norway’s Erling Haaland had crazy lore before he became a World Cup star
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a jam-packed episode. First, some updates about the World Cup. Then, a deep dive into the lore of Norway’s star player, Erling Haaland, from his Snapchat and YouTube vlogs, to his shopping trips in Dallas for cowboy hats, to his Birkin bags, to his cool soccer-playing girlfriend Isabel Haugseng Johansen.
Then, an update on some compressed flowers to debate: Lions wide receiver Amon-Ra St. Brown and Brooklyn Adams’ engagement, and Falcons wide receiver Drake London and Sofia Rose Gabay’s engagement.

entry.title: [SGS] Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding recap: Tom Brady’s paparazzi photo and Mike Vrabel’s PR tour
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a Tayvis Wedding Extravaganza! But first: The United States dabbled in FIFA corruption, which means America is officially a soccer country. Donald Trump called FIFA president, Gianni Infantino, and got Flo Balogun’s red card un-suspended (the gals recorded this before the game last night, but they did have a feeling it wouldn’t go well). They also talk about Norway’s Erling Haaland, the great Messi, and who will win it all.
Then it’s Tayvis time! The gals talk about the sportiest guests at the wedding, from Mike Vrabel, to Tom Brady, to Dean Blandino — and if you don’t know who Dean Blandino is, keep listening. The gals also discuss the business of Taylor Swift, the logistics of a wedding like this, Lena Dunham’s football speech, and much, much more!

entry.title: [SGS] Is Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s MSG wedding partly commentary on the nature of celebrity?
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with an episode about all the glimpses they’ve caught so far surrounding Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding.
But first, a few things! Serena Williams reportedly injured her knee during her Wimbledon singles match and is doubtful to play doubles with her sister, Venus. World Cupdate: Mexico made history and won the team’s first knockout match in 40 years when they beat Ecuador 2-0.
Then: LeBron is leaving the Lakers! He’s not retiring and he’ll play somewhere else. Will that be the Golden State Warriors with Steph Curry and Draymond Green? The Cleveland Cavaliers? No one knows yet. He did, however, get his son paid by LA before he bounced.
And finally: Taylor and Travis’s wedding! Taylor’s team applied for a street activity permit from July 2nd -July 4th near MSG — is this actually happening? The gals discuss everything we know so far and give their take on why the rollout of info could actually be a brilliant meta-commentary on Taylor’s public image to date.

But first, a few things! Serena Williams reportedly injured her knee during her Wimbledon singles match and is doubtful to play doubles with her sister, Venus. World Cupdate: Mexico made history and won the team’s first knockout match in 40 years when they beat Ecuador 2-0.

Then: LeBron is leaving the Lakers! He’s not retiring and he’ll play somewhere else. Will that be the Golden State Warriors with Steph Curry and Draymond Green? The Cleveland Cavaliers? No one knows yet. He did, however, get his son paid by LA before he bounced.

And finally: Taylor and Travis’s wedding! Taylor’s team applied for a street activity permit from July 2nd -July 4th near MSG — is this actually happening? entry.title: [SGS] All the World Cup Round of 32 gossip: Ronaldo’s fiancée falls for AI drama and a Norway hot take
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are thrilled to welcome Hannah Vanbiber to the show to talk all things World Cup for the Round of 32! Hannah is the co-host of the The Athletic FC Daily Cup on Amazon Fire and writer of The Athletic’s daily World Cup newsletter, and an absolute joy to chat with.
The gals and Hannah talk about the craziness of the Paraguay-Germany match, the vibes of the Brazil team, how the USMNT’s chances look against Bosnia and Herzegovina, who’s the most interesting player on the USMNT, the juiciest off-the-field story, and the wildest World Cup story you may not have heard of. Main characters include Erling Haaland, Florian Balogun, Cristiano Ronaldo, Alex Rodriguez, and many more.
Plus: a tease for Thursday’s episode about Taylor and Travis’s wedding!

entry.title: [SGS] “America’s Sweethearts” reveals the darkest truths about Dallas Cowboys cheerleaders and our culture
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a deep dive of Season 3 of “America's Sweethearts: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders.” The gals talked about Season 2 last year when it came out, which was a very different series that followed the cheerleaders as they fought for better pay.
This year, the antagonist is the outside world, and the show became a show about the show itself.
The gals analyze all of the themes the show presents: Supporting hegemonic masculinity, the Cowboys’ thin, white standard of beauty, the mirage of empowerment, what it means to be famous today, and this season as contextualized by the rise of the tradwife.
Plus: the origins of cheerleading, Reece’s decision to retire, Kelli and Judy leading from a place of fear, the hip surgeries that multiple performers need by the age of 25, the power imbalance that occurs when the show anoints certain women as stars, and much more.
Selected sources from this episode: Sekani Robinson, Black Ballerinas: The Management of Emotional and Aesthetic Labor (2021) La'Tonya Rease Miles, American Beauty: The Cheerleader in American Literature and Popular Culture (2005) Sophie Gilbert, Girl on Girl (2025) Laura Grindstaff, Emily West, Cheerleading and the Gendered Politics of Sport (2006) Alex Kuczynski, Beauty Junkies (2006) Michaele L. Ferguson, Choice Feminism and the Fear of Politics (2010)
0:00 The gals welcome us in 4:05 Fashion mailbag 7:48 America’s Sweethearts deep dive 19:04 Beauty standards on the DCC 28:37 DCC makeover day 38:21 The mirage of empowerment 52:21 Reece’s retirement and impact

entry.title: [SGS] Is Fox Sports purposely playing up the Alexi Lalas and Zlatan Ibrahimović drama?
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a jam-packed episode! First up: For Father’s Day, Gisele Bündchen posted about Joaquim Valente, her new husband and father of her youngest child, but left out her ex-husband Tom Brady. Yikes! Eagles star DeVonta Smith married Mya Danielle and set a new standard for compressed flowers. Former college basketball player Haley Cavinder, on the other hand, married Jake Ferguson of the Cowboys, and it was just standard compression.
The Milwaukee Bucks traded Giannis Antetokounmpo to the Heat, even though the Celtics appear to have offered up Jaylen Brown — which is a little awkward for Jaylen! Also, the gals don’t think this will work for the Heat, sorry to Miami. The Fever’s Myisha Hines-Allen was ejected after a team-wide Fever scuffle against the Mercury, and Caitlin Clark got her fifth tech of the season — there’s a lot of history between Mercury star DeWanna Bonner and the Fever.
Serena Williams is competing in women’s singles at Wimbledon, and former Wimbledon champ & World No. 6 Marketa Vondrousova is suspended from professional tennis until 2030 after refusing to take an anti-doping test in December 2025.
Now, World Cup stuff! Scottish fans are obsessed with Boston and drank all the beer in the city. The Fox Sports drama between Alexi Lalas and Zlatan Ibrahimović is putting host Rebecca Lowe in a very bad position. Norway beat Senegal, thanks to two goals from Erling Haaland, and all the Norwegian fans all pretend to row in the stands! There seem to be some issues in Senegal’s camp — there’s political upheaval in the country, so buckle up for a current events lesson. Curaçao's (playing Ivory Coast Thursday) Eloy Room recorded 15 saves against Ecuador, and Argentina’s Lionel Messi became the highest goalscorer in men's World Cup history on Monday (with France’s Kylian Mbappé close on his heels!).
0:00 The gals say hello 4:28 Gisele’s Father’s Day post 11:55 Compressed flower roundup 22:18 Giannis trade 26:25 Serena returns, Marketa suspended 31:41 World Cup stories

The Milwaukee Bucks traded Giannis Antetokounmpo to the Heat, even though the Celtics appear to have offered up Jaylen Brown — which is a little awkward for Jaylen! Also, the gals don’t think this will work for the Heat, sorry to Miami. The Fever’s Myisha Hines-Allen was ejected after a team-wide Fever scuffle against the Mercury, and Caitlin Clark got her fifth tech of the season — there’s a lot of history between Mercury star DeWanna Bonner and the Fever.

Serena Williams is competing in women’s singles at Wimbledon, and former Wimbledon champ & World No. 6 Marketa Vondrousova is suspended from professional tennis until 2030 after refusing to take an anti-doping test in December 2025.

Now, World Cup stuff! Scottish fans are obsessed with Boston and drank all the beer in the city. The Fox Sports drama between Alexi Lalas and Zlatan Ibrahimović is putting host Rebecca Lowe in a very bad position. Norway beat Senegal, thanks to two goals from Erling Haaland, and all the Norwegian fans all pretend to row in the stands! There seem to be some issues in Senegal’s camp — there’s political upheaval in the country, so buckle up for a current events lesson. Curaçao's (playing Ivory Coast Thursday) Eloy Room recorded 15 saves against Ecuador, and Argentina’s Lionel Messi became the highest goalscorer in men's World Cup history on Monday (with France’s Kylian Mbappé close on his heels!).

entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with everything you want to know about the USMNT players as you watch them. Who are they dating? What club do they play for? Where are they from? Info on Christian Pulisic, Tim Weah, Tim Ream, Weston Mckennie, Florian Balogun, Alex Freeman, and more.
And then: A deep dive into the feud between two royal American families of soccer. Seb Berhalter and Gio Reyna both play for the USMNT, but did you know that their families used to be super close? Until Gregg became Gio’s coach and Gio’s parents, Claudio and Danielle, got involved, that is. You don’t want to miss this one!

entry.title: [SGS] World Cup gossip: Cape Verde’s 40-year-old goalie, Iran’s complications, and Tunisia’s new hot coach
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back, reporting from a city still buzzing after the Knicks emerged victorious from the Finals. The boys are being silly on TV, KAT shouted out Jordyn Woods and her lucky bag, the gals decide that Mikal Bridges is the only player allowed to go live, and the parade is set for Thursday.
For all you hockey fans out there: The Carolina Hurricanes took the Stanley Cup with a winning combo of a nerd in the front office and a jock leading the charge on the ice. If you don’t know what that means, listen to the show!
Then, the gals dive into the World Cup, which is already sucking them in with the never-ending intrigue. Cape Verde is the story of the Group Stage so far, with its 40-year-old goalie, Vozinha, who saved the day in a draw against heavily favored Spain. Plus, one of Cape Verde’s players was invited to join the team via LinkedIn. The US beat Paraguay on Friday, and the gals will have a deep dive episode into the USMNT on Thursday. Tunisia fired their manager after the first game and hired a notoriously hot French coach, Herve Renard.
And finally: Some details and info about the Iranian national team’s very strange and complicated situation, and why their best player was left off the roster.
0:00 The gals say hello 0:18 The Knicks are NBA Champions 16:55 Hurricanes win Stanley Cup 24:07 World Cup highlights

entry.title: [SGS] The Knicks’ insane Game 4 comeback is a model for all leaders
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a TedTalk — sorry, episode — about the why the Knicks are modeling great leadership and how the team is winning despite a ton of distractions. The gals talk about what it was like to be in the building for the insane Game 4 comeback, the celebrities they saw, and the passion of fans who couldn’t be in the building.
Then they go on a deep dive into how the Knicks are exhibiting the psychological traits needed to win it all. From Karl-Anthony Towns and Jose Alvarado’s press conference, to Jalen Brunson’s ability to believe, to OG Anunoby’s incredible game-winner, to coach Mike Brown’s embracing of adversity, New York is behaving like a true team with great leadership.
They can visualize success, and they should be a lesson for everyone who is in charge of (or wants to make a meaningful difference within) an organization.
0:00 The gals serenade us 00:59 NBA Finals Game 3 reactions 19:07 Fashion mailbag 22:51 Deep dive into the Knicks’ success

entry.title: [SGS] Trump messed up the vibes at Knicks-Spurs Game 3: A behind-the-scenes recap of the NBA Finals
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a normal episode from Brooklyn after a whirlwind trip to San Antonio and Madison Square Garden for Games 1 and 3 of the NBA Finals. They talk about what it was like staying at the same hotel as all of ESPN’s major broadcasters in Texas, the state of vibes in San Antonio, and how crazy New York City was after the Game 2 win on Saturday.
Then, the gals take listeners behind the scenes of Game 3, which was the first Finals game at Madison Square Garden since 1999. The whole operation was messed up thanks to Donald Trump. He decided he was going to show up, after being invited by Knicks owner James Dolan, and make everyone’s lives miserable by causing a massive disruption to Manhattan and at the Garden.
The vibes were definitely skewed by his presence, but the Knicks did their best to fight them off. The Spurs ultimately won — is this a harbinger of things to come? The gals take Shareholders to the Garden for a play-by-play of the most memorable night in New York in decades.
0:00 The gals are back home! 2:44 Stories from San Antonio 17:20 Game 3 Recap 37:49 Predictions for the rest of the finals

entry.title: [SGS] The ultimate Knicks-Spurs Finals preview with DeAndre Jordan, Monty McCutchen, and Will Reeve
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with Part II of their NBA Finals extravaganza! The Sports Gossip Show was honored to be one of nine podcasts that the NBA invited to their first-ever Podcast Row. This episode is the second of two shows made up of interviews the gals conducted against the backdrop of the Spurs’ arena before Game 1.
First up is DeAndre Jordan, current NBA player, NBA All Star, and Finals champion. He talked about what it takes to be a good teammate, the wildest prank that was ever pulled on him in a locker room, and what it feels like to be traded and then have to play a game immediately with your new team. Then, a conversation with Head of Referee Training & Development, Monty McCutchen, who tells the gals how refs are chosen for the Finals, and much more. Finally, the gals talk to Will Reeve, who is covering the Finals for Good Morning America, about the spirit in New York City when the Knicks are this good.
A huge thank you to the NBA for making this possible!

entry.title: [SGS] The ultimate Knicks-Spurs Finals preview with Gary Payton, Malika Andrews, and more
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with one of the most exciting episodes yet before the NBA Finals kick off! The Sports Gossip Show was honored to be one of nine podcasts that the NBA invited to their first-ever Podcast Row. This episode is the first of two shows made up of observations from the Finals — and then interviews the gals conducted against the backdrop of the Spurs’ arena before Game 1.
The first guest is ABC/ESPN broadcaster Malika Andrews, who talks about sitting down with Karl-Anthony Towns, Jalen Brunson, and Victor Wembenyama, what it takes to work on the national stage, and how she decides who she thinks will win it all. Then, the gals talk to two dear friends of the show from The Athletic, Fred Katz and Jared Weiss. Fred spills some tea from his forthcoming book about the resurgence of the Knicks, and Jared talks about getting to know Wembenyama and the Spurs in his year covering the team. Fred also tells an unhinged story about Josh Hart and the candy Mike & Ikes.
Then, last but certainly not least, the gals sit down with NBA legend Gary Payton. He talks about why he won’t retire his number for anyone but the Seattle Supersonics, what it’s like having his son play in the league, and then he completes an absolutely ridiculous task that Madeline and Charlotte throw his way.
You don’t want to miss this one. The gals want to send a huge thanks to the NBA for this opportunity — and stayed tuned for Part II!
0:00 The gals have a serious message 1:45 We’re at Podcast Row at the NBA Finals! 10:33 ESPN’s Malika Andrews 31:02 The Athletic’s Fred Katz and Jared Weiss 49:10 NBA legend Gary Payton

entry.title: [SGS] Why NHL fans hate the Vegas Golden Knights with DJ Bean
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are thrilled to be joined by DJ Bean of the daily show “What Chaos” to talk about the NHL Playoffs and Stanley Cup Final, as many of the Shareholders have been asking for!
But first: A couple updates from the NBA Finals. The Oklahoma City Thunder are up on the Spurs, 3-2, and the winner of the series will face the New York Knicks. The Cleveland Cavaliers are keeping Kenny Atkinson as their coach and are re-signing James Harden to a multi-year deal. Huh! Trump may go to the NBA Finals in New York, and the transportation situation at Madison Square Garden is going to be very interesting if the Knicks have a home game and the World Cup starts on the same day.
Then: Stanley Cup Final stuff! DJ joins to talk about why fans hate the Vegas Golden Knights, why the GM of the Hurricanes is doing things no one has done before, whether the Canadiens have a chance, and the Nerd candy situation that Seth Jarvis has found himself in.
0:00 The gals welcome us in 3:02 NBA playoffs update 20:23 Fashion mailbag 24:18 DJ Bean talks Stanley Cup Final

entry.title: [SGS] How the New York Knicks are playing with the most immaculate vibes we’ve ever seen
entry.summary:
A little housekeeping: SGS merch is now available for purchase!
Madeline and Charlotte are back with another episode from New York City, which is alive with Knicks excitement. It’s hard to believe the Knicks are back in the Finals after 27 years, but it gets easier when you consider how immaculate the team’s vibes are. The gals talk about the Knicks’ stars, from Jalen Brunson to Karl-Anthony Towns, and the WAGs, from Jordyn Woods to Timothée Chalamet, and superfans, from Spike Lee to the ghost of JFK Jr. This is the sweet spot for Knicks fans!
Then, a few things: Giants’ QB Jaxson Dart introduced Donald Trump at a rally (yikes), Patriots offensive tackle Will Campbell is engaged to Ashlynn Nussmeier (the sister of Will’s former teammate Garrett Nussmeier), we got more photos from AJ Brown’s wedding, and Pep Guardiola’s estranged wife, Cristina Serra, went to watch his last match as Manchester City’s manager.

entry.content:
A little housekeeping: SGS merch is now available for purchase!

Madeline and Charlotte are back with another episode from New York City, which is alive with Knicks excitement. It’s hard to believe the Knicks are back in the Finals after 27 years, but it gets easier when you consider how immaculate the team’s vibes are. The gals talk about the Knicks’ stars, from Jalen Brunson to Karl-Anthony Towns, and the WAGs, from Jordyn Woods to Timothée Chalamet, and superfans, from Spike Lee to the ghost of JFK Jr. This is the sweet spot for Knicks fans!

Then, a few things: Giants’ QB Jaxson Dart introduced Donald Trump at a rally (yikes), Patriots offensive tackle Will Campbell is engaged to Ashlynn Nussmeier (the sister of Will’s former teammate Garrett Nussmeier), we got more photos from AJ Brown’s wedding, and Pep Guardiola’s estranged wife, Cristina Serra, went to watch his last match as Manchester City’s manager.

18:17 Celebrity Knicks fans
29:33 Cavaliers implode

48:17 Pep Guardiola’s estranged wife at his last game

entry.title: [SGS] Victor Wembanyama is the sports enigma that Aaron Rodgers wishes he was
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with an episode about a bunch of SGS main characters, but first: They start with the Western Conference Finals! The Thunder and the Spurs played a game that was more like Shakespeare than a basketball competition. San Antonio’s young star, Victor Wembanyama, is doing things on the court never seen before. He is also an enigma and has reached spiritual heights most of us only dream of.
Then: Tom Brady walked in a Gucci show that Alix Earle was at, José Mourinho is returning to Real Madrid as head coach and Pep Guardiola will leave his role as Manchester City manager this summer, Aaron Rodgers is returning to Steelers, NBA Rookie of the Year Cooper Flagg hard launched girlfriend and Duke University center, Arianna Roberson, Taylor and Travis were in Bushwick, and Eagles star AJ Brown got married to Kelsey Riley at Montage Laguna Beach. Were the flowers compressed? Listen to find out!

entry.title: [SGS] Real Madrid is a mess: unhinged press conferences and teammates fighting while Kylian Mbappé laughs
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a real wild ride of an episode that starts with Tom Brady’s Mother’s Day posts. Then, NBA Playoff gossip: LeBron and the Lakers are out, so what will he do next? Stay in LA? Play for the Cleveland Cavaliers again? Retire? The Knicks’ vibes are insanely good and Philly got absolutely destroyed by a Josh Hart zinger. Plus: Daryl Morey is out as the 76ers president of basketball operations.
Luka Doncic put out an icky press release about his custody battle, Portland Fire upset NY Liberty at the buzzer, and F1 is taking employee feedback. And, finally: Real Madrid is an absolute disaster: Federico Valverde and Aurelien Tchouameni are fighting each other, Kylian Mbappé is laughing, and club president Florentino Perez held the most unhinged press conference the gals have ever seen. Which is saying something.

entry.title: [SGS] Do Caitlin Clark and Morgan Wallen actually need each other?
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with another deep dive, this time into Caitlin Clark and Morgan Wallen. The WNBA star recently walked out with the country singer at his concert in Indianapolis, and some fans are angry that she would associate herself with the controversial musician.
The gals go down a rabbit hole of Caitlin’s history of saying or not saying things when it comes to denouncing racism that some of her fans hurl at other WNBA players. They talk about Caitlin’s attempts at being apolitical and why those fail. Then they dive into Morgan Wallen’s history of being an athlete, the number of controversies he’s gotten himself into, and all the athletes he’s brought out at his concerts, including scandal-plagued guys like Mike Tyson, Brett Favre, Hulk Hogan, and more.
This episode even includes a deep dive into the tax records of Morgan’s charity and his attempts to sportswash his image through philanthropy. The gals then answer the question: What if Caitlin Clark and Morgan Wallen actually need each other?
0:00 The gals welcome us in! 2:39 Caitlin Clark’s history 17:55 Morgan Wallen’s history 41:10 Sportswashing and Morgan’s charity

entry.title: [SGS] WNBA season preview: which players are dating each other, WNBA-NBA couples, and off-season drama
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a WNBA season preview that only *they* could come up with: All the offseason drama due to the new collective bargaining agreement, and then a whole section about which players are dating each other and which players are part of WNBA-NBA couples.
The gals talk about the new expansion teams in Portland and Toronto, how the Aces and Liberty will be hard to beat, how A’ja Wilson will probably be a five-time MVP at the end of the season, why Angel Reese left Chicago, which GMs would take Paige Bueckers over Caitlin Clark, Lynx star Napheesa Collier and Lynx coach Cheryl Reeve’s coordinated PR campaign, and much more.
Then, relationships! A few include: Dewanna Bonner and Alyssa Thomas, Breanna Stewart and her wife Marta, Caitlin Clark and Connor McCaffery, and Natasha Cloud and Isabelle Harrison. Also — Dijoani Carrington and Nalyssa Smith broke up and now people think that Dijonai is dating Jackie Young. Plus: A’ja and Bam Adebayo went to the GQ Met Gala after party together in coordinating custom outfits, Angel and Magic’s Wendell Carter Jr., Kiki Rice and OKC’s Cason Wallace, and why it’s a great idea that Kelsey Plum and Napheesa Collier ended up with high-level athletic trainers.
0:00 Welcome to the 2026 WNBA preview! 4:26 New WNBA teams 10:13 Established WNBA teams 36:41 WNBA relationships

entry.title: [SGS] The MET Gala proves that sports have never been more important in pop culture
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a full discussion about this year’s MET Gala and why it clearly shows that sports have never been more important in the broader cultural conversation. Athlete attendees this year included Eileen Gu, Alysa Liu, Lindsey Vonn, Jimmy Butler, Joe Burrow, Justin Jefferson, Paige Bueckers, Angel Reese, A'ja Wilson, Ciara and Russell Wilson, Gabrielle Union and Dwayne Wade, Serena and Venus Williams, Naomi Osaka, and many more.
Then, a rundown of current NBA Playoff drama: The Celtics are out and both Joe Mazzulla and Jaylen Brown are coping, Steve Kerr and Draymond Green are fighting, the Knicks are looking good, LeBron is indefatiguable, and Victor Wembanyama continues to raise the bar for male emotional intelligence in sports.

entry.title: [SGS] Jennifer Welch says sports can’t heal America
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are thrilled to be joined by Jennifer Welch, host of the “I’ve Had It” podcast with Angie “Pumps” Sullivan. Jennifer is an interior designer from Oklahoma City-turned probably the most influential progressive media personality today. She is the author of the book “Not Today, Fascists: The Lies That Got Us Here, and the Truths That Will Reunite Us” which will be out in the fall, and regularly interviews the most prominent politicians in the country.
The gals talk to Jennifer about how a Bravo show led her and Pumps to start their podcast, the grief that comes with deconstructing right-wing political beliefs, why Jennifer doesn’t think sports will bring us all together, her love for the Oklahoma City Thunder and Rafael Nadal, and much, much more!

entry.title: [SGS] The Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini scandal keeps getting worse: Updated timeline and analysis
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a complete timeline of the Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini scandal. More pictures came out Thursday, Vrabel gave a horrible press conference, and this is now a story about some of the worst PR of all time. For those of you who are new here, the gals explain everything, and for those of you who’ve been following along, they’ve got fresh analysis.

entry.title: [SGS] Mike Vrabel finally speaks out
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with a new ep! First, relationship news: Guardians catcher Austin Hedges proposed on the field after win, Anna Hall married Giants wide receiver Darius Slayton, Gracie Hunt’s non-engagement party party, Sam Darnold’s wife Katie Hoofnagle Darnold gave the exclusive to Hailee Steinfeld’s newsletter Beau Society.
Tom Brady posted a shirtless Reel playing volleyball with his daughter. As one does! Mike Vrabel spoke to reporters about the Dianna Russini situation and the statement was as good as we’re going to get.
NBA Playoff drama —is this the end of the Warriors dynasty? Cleveland put out a questionable graphic. LeBron and his son Bronny did a promo for Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey, and Charlotte gives what is hopefully a correct history lesson. The Portland Trailblazers’ new owner, a guy named Tom Dundon, is super cheap?? The gals dive in.
And, finally, two other lessons in PR: Azzi Fudd and Paige Bueckers and the Dallas Wings communication people, and Lando Norris and the Guardian.

entry.title: [SGS] Why the response to the Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini story wouldn’t happen to a man
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with some updates: After photos leaked of NFL insider Dianna Russini holding hands with Patriots head coach, Mike Vrabel, Russini resigned from The Athletic. The gals talk about how male insiders in this industry cross ethical lines constantly and are not held to the same scrutiny and don’t suffer the same consequences. Plus: What’s going on with Mike Vrabel and the Patriots?
UNC football head coach Bill Belichick and his girlfriend, Jordon Hudson, attended a Savannah Bananas game. Speaking of UNC, former Denver Nuggets Michael Malone is the new head basketball coach, and his daughter is also a volleyball player at the school. Eagles offensive tackle Lane Johnson got married to Kelsey Kay Holmer — were the flowers compressed? Bears quarterback Caleb Williams was spotted at Coachella with Kendall and Kylie Jenner, which is sports.
WNBA free agency is in full swing and the gals have updates about Azzi Fudd, Satou Sabally, A’ja Wilson, and some damning stuff about the Chicago Sky. Amazon had “technical difficulties” during the very end of the very close, over time Hornets-Heat NBA play-in tournament game. Luka Doncic and Austin Reeves are out “indefinitely” for the Lakers with injuries, and Doc Rivers is out as the Bucks coach.

entry.title: [SGS] Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel: everyone is missing the point
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back after a short break last week to tackle the story everyone is talking about: The photos of NFL insider Dianna Russini and Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel. The gals talk about the public relations nature of the situation, the role that “insider” reporters play in sports, and the bigger picture of the media landscape that people need to consider when talking about the situation.
First, a few items about the WNBA Draft (more on that on Thursday), because former UConn teammates Azzi Fudd and Paige Bueckers were reunited on the Dallas Wings and…are they still dating? Rory McIlroy won the Masters for the second year in a row, and now he has to come up with a whole new menu for the special dinner. Plus: the Artemis II astronauts are the GOAT sports figures of our time.

entry.title: [SGS] We found a shocking new development in the Athlete Wedding Industrial Complex
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are back with another episode — the only one for this week because they are taking a tiny break! First, a few March Madness updates: UCLA won the women’s tournament and the Michigan men beat UConn with five transfers. Geno Auriemma went after Dawn Staley and the apology he issued was…not great!
The Masters starts on Thursday and golfer Jason Day is making waves with his Malbon outfit, which involves birds and fish (complimentary). Plus: Rory McIlroy, Scottie Scheffler’s nine-day-old son, and more. Tiger Woods is going to rehab in Switzerland? Bills quarterback Josh Allen and his wife, actress Hailee Steinfeld, had their baby and went to Seahawks Quarterback Sam Darnold’s wedding. Gracie Hunt (daughter of Kansas City Chiefs owner Clark Hunt) had a compressed flower engagement that exposed a new problem in the athlete wedding industrial complex: votives. Track star Noah Lyles got married to Junelle Bromfield, a Jamaican track and field athlete, at the same place NBA star Kemba Walker got married.
Angel Reese is being traded from the Chicago Sky to the Atlanta Dream, which is great for Atlanta and stupid for Chicago. Italy didn’t make their third-straight World Cup, Cleveland Browns head coach Todd Monken missed the NFL coach’s photo because he was getting his haircut for the photo, and Lakers star Luka Doncic is going to Spain to deal with an injury.
0:00 The gals are in different states! 6:26 March Madness finishes 22:36 The Masters preview 36:56 Josh and Hailee welcome their baby 39:42 Gracie Hunt’s engagement 44:42 Noah Lyles and Junelle Bromfield’s wedding 46:32 Angel Reese to Atlanta 49:20 Italy misses World Cup 50:14 NFL coaches photo 52:30 Luka’s treatment

entry.title: [SGS] Why Morgan Riddle embraces the term WAG
entry.summary:
Madeline and Charlotte are thrilled to welcome Morgan Riddle to the show! The tennis influencer, content creator, co-founder of The 400 Club, and girlfriend of tennis star Taylor Fritz joins the gals for a wide-ranging conversation about where she came from, how she conquered the tennis world, and where she’s going next.
They chat about how Morgan grew up going to hockey games in Minnesota, wore heels to high school, went to Wagner College, and then moved to LA, where she met Taylor. She talks about the first time she went viral, why she quit her job to become a full-time influencer, why she and Taylor deliberately don’t work together, and her relationship with her stylist who she met when she was 13. Morgan also describes the process of choosing what she wears to matches, why she decides to speak out against injustice on social media, and how she’s seen the tennis world dress differently since she started going on tour. She also talks about the worst sporting event experience she ever had and which sports she’d love to go to more often.
Then Morgan talks about The 400 Club, the company of which she’s a co-founder. She wants to give women places that feel safe to watch sports and help brands understand how to reach female fans in ways they haven’t thought of before.
0:00 We’re in a studio! 2:09 Welcome Morgan! 3:31 Morgan’s background 8:39 Tennis’ audience 11:51 Morgan and Taylor’s parallel careers 13:10 Interview with NY Times Fashion 19:00 Vintage picks 21:23 Interaction amongst players 23:18 Brand partnerships 24:42 Authenticity on socials 26:09 The 400 Club 36:45 Raya songs 37:55 Favorite sports gossip story
